subject: Discrete Random Variables
topic: §16 Expected Value of a Function of a Discrete Random Variable
question: Show that $E(X^2) = E(X(X - 1)) + E(X)$.
explanation: Let $D$ be the range of $X$. We have $E(X^2) = \sum_{x \in D} x^2 p(x) = \sum_{x \in D} (x(x - 1) + x) p(x) = \sum_{x \in D} x(x - 1) p(x) + \sum_{x \in D} x p(x) = E(X(X - 1)) + E(X)$
